summary: Container-job contracts, workflow code, API/service units, image-acquisition locking, private-image authorization, and generic reliability/CI foundations exist on main, but #3266's authoritative production-boundary validation suite does not. HEAD equals origin/main at ffa18ecf, no recent commit directly references #3266 or its title, the sole ContainerJob Temporal integration test stubs every activity, and no small-image, .NET execution, combined Omnigent, local-registry, restart/cancellation, repeated-workflow cache, or scheduled large-image journey is present. New test/fixture/CI work is required; no long suites were run.

requirements.description: A PR-safe smoke journey traverses authenticated API/MCP → durable job record → Temporal → trusted worker → configured Docker proxy/system daemon → durable evidence/cleanup. Not present: tests/integration/workflows/temporal/test_container_job_workflow.py starts the workflow directly and replaces all production activities with local stubs.

A .NET test uses the same generic job contract and backend path as the smoke test. tests/integration/temporal/test_integration_ci_tool_contract.py proves Alpine and .NET requests share a schema, but it does not execute either workload through the backend journey.

A combined Omnigent journey discovers/calls the tools against its authorized worktree without direct Docker authority. Existing Omnigent integration tests exercise bridge/fake-server behavior, not a combined-stack container job journey.

Concurrent requests for one absent image produce one pull and multiple successful cache consumers. tests/unit/workflows/temporal/test_container_image_acquisition.py covers locking, waiter re-inspection, and one simulated pull, but no production-boundary concurrent job journey exists.
A later independent workflow reuses the image after the first workflow/session ends. Cache-hit behavior is unit tested, but no two-workflow/session production journey proves daemon-level reuse.

Private-registry tests prove per-run authorization on a cache hit and no secret leakage. Unit tests cover cached-image denial, ephemeral auth cleanup, and redaction-oriented contracts, but there is no authenticated local-registry integration journey.

Cancellation/timeout/restart tests preserve one job/container identity, partial evidence, terminal state, and idempotent cleanup. Focused workflow/service tests cover portions of cancellation and cleanup, but no controlled API/worker restart or evidence-publication journey covers the full requirement.

Scheduled large-image/Unreal validation proves the second workflow does not pull again and uses no toolchain-specific backend infrastructure. No such canary test, manifest, workflow, or durable evidence publisher was found.

Job cleanup never removes the shared image; image maintenance remains separately controlled. The backend and focused tests reflect container-scoped cleanup and separate image acquisition behavior, but the requested journey-level assertion is absent.

Architecture assertions prove no managed/Omnigent test runtime receives a socket, endpoint, DOCKER_HOST, sidecar, or raw bind path. Focused managed-session/schema tests assert several no-Docker boundaries, but no assertion spans every requested journey/runtime and combined-stack topology.

Changes to critical service seams select required journey coverage through CI according to #3146. A reliability_journey selector and CI job exist, but they currently target checkpoint/replay paths and do not provide or comprehensively select #3266's container-job journeys.

Failures leave bounded, redacted, reviewable evidence with job/workflow/session correlation. Production schemas/services and the generic reliability foundation contain correlation and redaction mechanisms, but no requested journey demonstrates diagnostics across each authority handoff.
